faqTemplateAnswers.Is this card lifetime free?: Yes, IndusInd Platinum Aura Edge has no joining fee and no annual fee.
faqTemplateAnswers.Is it worth it?: It's a reasonable entry-level free card if you like its unique 'choose your plan' (Home/Shop/Travel/Party) reward structure, but the base reward rate of 0.5 points per ₹100 on non-accelerated categories is low, and it lacks lounge access or other lifestyle perks.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should get this card?: First-time credit cardholders who want a no-fee card with customizable rewards aligned to one dominant spending pattern (groceries, shopping, travel, or dining).
faqTemplateAnswers.Does it support UPI?: No. This card is issued on the Visa/Mastercard network, not RuPay, so it cannot be linked to UPI apps; IndusInd offers a separate Platinum RuPay Credit Card for UPI-linked spending.
faqTemplateAnswers.Can I get lounge access with this card?: No, this card does not offer any airport or railway lounge access.
faqTemplateAnswers.What income is required to qualify?: As an entry-level card, IndusInd generally applies relaxed eligibility criteria; exact income thresholds are not publicly standardized and depend on the bank's internal assessment.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should avoid this card?: High spenders wanting lounge access, movie benefits, or a higher blanket reward rate should look elsewhere, since this card's base reward rate is low outside your chosen plan's top category, and redemption is capped monthly.
faqTemplateAnswers.What are the best alternatives?: Axis Bank ACE Credit Card or HDFC Millennia Credit Card offer more consistent cashback/reward value.

categories.label: Top category in chosen plan (e.g. groceries, hotels, dining)
